_meta.note: PARSER-GENERATED. Validate by diff against hand-derived records.

records.name: SHIFT
records.rawExplanation: *2021–2022 (hypergrowth compounder):* SHIFT was the classic beat-and-raise software-services compounder — software QA/testing built on the CAT aptitude tool and a fast-scaling engineer headcount, recurring project revenue compounding ~30%+ a year. Management raised guidance repeatedly; the market paid a rich growth multiple and the stock ran to its multi-year highs. Business strong, stock strong → Q1. (Reads as the existing menu: sustained growth / beat-and-raise.)

records.name: SHIFT

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2023 (growth normalises, multiple compresses):* revenue kept growing but the rate decelerated off hypergrowth toward the mid-teens, and the rich multiple began to compress — a de-rating of a former high-flyer rather than any business stumble. Business still ◎ (growing), stock ✕ (multiple compression) → Q2. Not a geographic or event story — a pure growth-stock re-rating.

records.name: SHIFT

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2024 (continued de-rating):* growth normalising further (consensus now models revenue ~16%/yr, earnings ~22%/yr), still healthy in absolute terms but no longer the 30%+ that justified the peak multiple. The stock kept grinding lower well off its highs as the valuation reset continued. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ✕ → Q2, same re-rating phase.

records.name: SHIFT

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2025 (strategic-investment margin trough):* the inverse split-signal year — revenue still growing but profit DOWN, because SHIFT front-loaded heavy AI investment (building the DQS modernization service), spiking SG&A. Q1 FY8/2026 operating profit fell 19.9% to ¥2.8bn, missing expectations. The stock cratered from the ~¥1,200 area (Oct 2025) toward ~¥666 (Apr 2026), roughly -49% over six months and ~-64% off the trailing-year high of ¥1,827.5. Revenue ◎ / profit ✕ (deliberate investment, not demand loss), stock ✕✕.

records.name: SHIFT

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2026 (investment-payoff inflection — under-priced recovery):* Q2 FY8/2026 (reported ~Apr 15 2026) printed record net sales +122% YoY (NOTE: almost certainly M&A/consolidation-inflated, not organic), gross margin improving to 34.4% from 32.5%, and the full-year revenue forecast raised to ¥160bn. The flagship AI service DQS generated ¥2.55bn within two months of launch; management guided to a "firm prospect of recovery" in H2 as the AI investments mature and SG&A normalises. The stock jumped 4.65% to ¥657.2 on the print — but is still down ~49% over six months and sits far below the ~¥1,208 average analyst target (4 buys, 0 sells, implied upside ~+81%). Business re-accelerating and guidance raised, but the stock has NOT caught up → Q2 (the under-priced / business-ahead-of-price quadrant). Reads partly as the existing menu (guidance raised / beat-and-raise) plus a new investment-payoff inflection. Bears: the headline growth is acquisition-flattered, AI-investment returns are unproven, and SG&A remains elevated until the payoff lands.
